---
title: "Disk drive not recognised"
date: 2008-01-15
forum: General Help
---

### Post by - Lazlo - on 2008-01-15
My disk drive is not being recognised any more.

It gives the following error:

```
mount: special device /dev/scd0 does not exist
```

sudo mount /dev/scd0 /media/cdrom0 gives the same error

Fstab looks like this:
```

# /etc/fstab: static file system information.
#
#  -- This file has been automaticly generated by ntfs-config --
#
#               

proc /proc proc defaults 0 0
# Entry for /dev/sda5 :
UUID=28fd7288-3f35-46c0-bfd1-a142e163cc5f / ext3 defaults,errors=remount-ro 0 1
# Entry for /dev/sda7 :
UUID=5187e58d-0e70-4184-b184-af033a5ca4c6 /home ext3 defaults 0 2
# Entry for /dev/sda1 :
UUID=8610189410188CF5 /windows ntfs-3g defaults,locale=nl_NL.UTF-8 0 1
# Entry for /dev/sda6 :
UUID=71982c77-b3c6-4aaa-83dc-44ab9181796c none swap sw 0 0
/dev/scd0 /media/cdrom0 udf,iso9660 user,noauto 0 0
```

All the other drives have UUID's, but my cdrom hasn't, could that be the problem.

I'm running on a fully upgraded Gutsy. Disk drive is a NEC DVD-RW AD-7173S S-ATA.

The problems started after the update to Gutsy, so I think it has something to do with those new UUID.

---

### Post by Omnios on 2008-01-15
Try this in the terminal
```

blkid

```
 It will show codes.

---

### Post by - Lazlo - on 2008-01-16
```

/dev/sda1: TYPE="ntfs" UUID="8610189410188CF5" 
/dev/sda5: UUID="28fd7288-3f35-46c0-bfd1-a142e163cc5f" SEC_TYPE="ext2" TYPE="ext3" 
/dev/sda6: TYPE="swap" UUID="71982c77-b3c6-4aaa-83dc-44ab9181796c" 
/dev/sda7: UUID="5187e58d-0e70-4184-b184-af033a5ca4c6" SEC_TYPE="ext2" TYPE="ext3" 

```

No disk drive mentioned.
